heading: SIPC advances

children.text: if all or any portion of the net equity claim of a customer in excess of his ratable share of customer property is a claim for cash, as distinct from a claim for securities or options on commodity futures contracts, the amount advanced to satisfy such claim for cash shall not exceed the standard maximum cash advance amount for each such customer, as determined in accordance with subsection (d);
a customer who holds accounts with the debtor in separate capacities shall be deemed to be a different customer in each capacity;

if all or any portion of the net equity claim of a customer in excess of his ratable share of customer property is satisfied by the delivery of securities purchased by the trustee pursuant to , the securities so purchased shall be valued as of the filing date for purposes of applying the dollar limitations of this subsection;section 78fff–2(d) of this title

no advance shall be made by SIPC to the trustee to pay or otherwise satisfy, directly or indirectly, any net equity claim of a customer who is a general partner, officer, or director of the debtor, a beneficial owner of five per centum or more of any class of equity security of the debtor (other than a nonconvertible stock having fixed preferential dividend and liquidation rights), a limited partner with a participation of five per centum or more in the net assets or net profits of the debtor, or a person who, directly or indirectly and through agreement or otherwise, exercised or had the power to exercise a controlling influence over the management or policies of the debtor; and

no advance shall be made by SIPC to the trustee to pay or otherwise satisfy any net equity claim of any customer who is a broker or dealer or bank, other than to the extent that it shall be established to the satisfaction of the trustee, from the books and rec­ords of the debtor or from the books and rec­ords of a broker or dealer or bank, or otherwise, that the net equity claim of such broker or dealer or bank against the debtor arose out of transactions for customers of such broker or dealer or bank (which customers are not themselves a broker or dealer or bank or a person described in paragraph (4)), in which event each such customer of such broker or dealer or bank shall be deemed a separate customer of the debtor.
such moneys as may be required to carry out ; andsection 78fff–2(e) of this title

to the extent the general estate of the debtor is not sufficient to pay any and all costs and expenses of administration of the estate of the debtor and of the liquidation proceeding, the amount of such costs and expenses.
pay or guarantee indebtedness of the debtor to a bank, lender, or other person under ;section 78fff–1(b)(2) of this title
guarantee or secure any indemnity under ; andsection 78fff–2(f) of this title
purchase securities under .section 78fff–2(d) of this title

children.heading: Standard maximum cash advance amount defined
children.label: d Standard maximum cash advance amount defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “standard maximum cash advance amount” means $250,000, as such amount may be adjusted after , as provided under subsection (e).December 31, 2010

children.label: A
children.text: $250,000 multiplied by—

children.label: B
children.text: the ratio of the annual value of the Personal Consumption Expenditures Chain-Type Price Index (or any successor index thereto), published by the Department of Commerce, for the calendar year preceding the year in which such determination is made, to the published annual value of such index for the calendar year preceding 2010.

children.heading: Rounding
children.label: 2 Rounding
children.text: If the standard maximum cash advance amount determined under paragraph (1) for any period is not a multiple of $10,000, the amount so determined shall be rounded down to the nearest $10,000.

children.heading: Publication and report to the Congress
children.label: 3 Publication and report to the Congress
A
children.text: the Commission shall publish in the Federal Register the standard maximum cash advance amount; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Board of Directors of SIPC shall submit a report to the Congress stating the standard maximum cash advance amount.

children.heading: Implementation period
children.label: 4 Implementation period
children.text: Any adjustment to the standard maximum cash advance amount shall take effect on January 1 of the year immediately succeeding the calendar year in which such adjustment is made.

children.label: A
children.text: the overall state of the fund and the economic conditions affecting members of SIPC;

children.label: B
children.text: the potential problems affecting members of SIPC; and

children.label: C
children.text: such other factors as the Board of Directors of SIPC may determine appropriate.
